summary:
The needle has been found and fixed! 

The problem was in the `informerUpdate` method of the `AssumeCache` struct in `main.go`. The original code would silently drop assumed objects when newer informer updates arrived, without notifying the callers who had made assumptions. 

My fix modified the `informerUpdate` method to properly set the `conflictNotified` flag when such conflicts occur, which makes the test pass and ensures that conflicts between assumed state and informer updates are properly reported instead of being silently ignored.

The test.sh script now outputs "OK: conflict notified" and exits with code 0, confirming that the fix works correctly.
